description: Tiered verb lexicon for D1 (verb observability) scoring in the SLO evaluation framework. Tier 0 = unobservable internal state; Tier 3 = precise, contextually specific observable performance. Based on Mager (1975) observability principle and Anderson & Krathwohl (2001) cognitive taxonomy.

0.label: Unobservable
0.description: Internal mental states that cannot be directly observed or assessed. Fundamental defect — an LO using these verbs cannot be evaluated.

1.label: Weak observable
1.description: Observable in principle but insufficiently specific to determine a clear assessment method. Common in Irish HE — often a legacy of pre-outcome-based curriculum writing.

2.label: Clear observable
2.description: Directly observable and assessable. A clear assessment method is implied. Represents solid LO writing.

3.label: Precise / contextual
3.description: Specifies a precise, contextually-situated performance. Typically implies both the action and the artefact or context. The LO almost writes its own assessment.

contextDependentVerbs.description: Verbs whose tier depends on usage context. Flag for LLM confirmation when detected.

understand.exception: If used as 'demonstrate an understanding of X by doing Y' where Y is Tier 2/3, score on Y not 'understand'

illustrate.exception: If used as 'produce an illustration of X' or 'draw a diagram of X', treat as Tier 2

recognise.exception: In clinical/diagnostic contexts ('recognise symptoms of X') where recognition IS the professional performance, treat as Tier 2

select.exception: If selection requires applying criteria ('select an appropriate algorithm based on...'), treat as Tier 2

implement.exception: If implementing a novel or self-designed solution, treat as Tier 3

investigate.exception: If investigating an original or open-ended question, treat as Tier 3

compoundPatterns.description: Patterns that trigger D2 (behavioral singularity) compound flag regardless of verb tiers.

instructorCenteredPatterns.description: Sentence-opening patterns that trigger D3 (student-centeredness) flag.
